name: Volt Typhoon (BRONZE SILHOUETTE)
description:
2026-05-18 | Active Pre-Positioning & LotL Activity
Target: Critical infrastructure Operational Technology (OT) networks in Singapore, focused heavily on port automation control systems supporting PSA and Jurong Port layouts.
Vector: Stealth-first Living-off-the-land (LotL) execution exploiting unpatched edge networking components to harvest internal domain credentials.
Impact: Silent establishment of persistent access footprints inside Terminal Operating Systems (TOS). In contrast to financial cybercrime, this deployment focuses strictly on preparing for potential infrastructure sabotage.

name: UNC3886 (Advanced Cyber Espionage)
description:
2026-05-04 | Edge Firewall Zero-Day Campaign
Target: Telecommunications backbones and core routing pathways servicing Singapore's maritime Critical Information Infrastructure (CII).
Vector: Deployment of sophisticated custom rootkits exploiting unauthenticated remote execution zero-days in perimeter security appliances.
Impact: Comprehensive mapping and sniffing of deep network traffic paths linking primary command cells to port operations, circumventing standard enterprise firewalls.

name: Akira Ransomware Group
description:
2026-05-12 | Vendor Supply Chain Extortion
Target: Third-party cloud-native Maritime Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) and fleet logistics software providers serving Singapore hub networks.
Vector: Helpdesk social engineering and high-frequency MFA fatigue attacks to compromise administrator portals.
Impact: Quiet data-theft campaign exfiltrating over 1TB of cargo manifests and custom clearance pipelines without deploying active encryption payloads, relying heavily on encryptionless extortion to avoid triggering local EDR responses.

name: The "Sorry" Ransomware Cartel
description:
2026-05-21 | Automated Web Panel Exploitation
Target: Mid-tier shipping logistics agencies, freight forwarders, and bunkering providers operating in Singapore port waters.
Vector: Automated scanning and exploit execution leveraging the critical cPanel/WHM authentication bypass flaw (CVE-2026-41940) to gain root shell control over web portals.
Impact: Widespread deployment of active file encryption payloads across web management engines, disabling customer-facing booking portals and logistics entry systems.

name: APT40 (Island Dreams, GINGKO PANDA)
description:
2026-05-28 | Navigational Supply Chain Infiltration
Target: Marine Satellite Communication (Satcom) infrastructure networks and Electronic Chart Display & Information Systems (ECDIS) vendors in the region.
Vector: Precision spear-phishing targeting shore-side marine technicians to place malicious updates inside firmware deployment channels.
Impact: Ongoing downstream delivery attempts to land backdoors inside vessel bridge networks to monitor active telemetry and routing coordinates.


2026-03-02 | Historical Log
Target: Local maritime OT integrators. Intercepted remote diagnostic tunnels to monitor engine management telemetry.

name: Handala Cyber-Espionage Group
description:
2026-05-08 | Geopolitical Transit Hub Infiltration
Target: Public data mirrors and third-party transit agency databases linked to global shipping lanes.
Vector: Structured credential harvesting paired with targeted application layer exploits.
Impact: Mass exfiltration of regional ship logs, customs profiles, and global transit schedules; primarily focused on geopolitical data gathering rather than destructive operational impact.
